Stretch target: Rating A = 531.15 KB and 0.0790 g per page.

Cross-Page Patterns

Recurring component and bundle opportunities that can often be fixed once in templates, CMS blocks, or the design system.

  • This same heavy image pattern appears on 99 pages. Fix it once in the shared template, CMS block, or design system component to reduce load across the whole site.
  • These 1 script bundles are recurring modularization targets. Move the fix to the shared bundle or component boundary so every affected page gets lighter at once.
  • These 3 modularization targets recur across multiple pages. Centralize the fix at the component or bundle level instead of treating each page separately.
  • Heavy assets from administracion.gob.es recur across 100 pages. Review whether that dependency should be deferred, optimized, or loaded conditionally.

Site-Wide Guidance

Shared recommendations grouped by site origin so platform-level fixes can be prioritized before page-by-page cleanup.

This scan appears to target a single site. Shared issues are usually best fixed once at template/platform level.

https://administracion.gob.es

Sampled pages: 100

  • [LOW] Publish .well-known/security.txt

    CSP is absent across all sampled pages on https://administracion.gob.es.
  • [HIGH] Introduce service worker support
    No sampled pages on https://administracion.gob.es indicate service worker support for offline resilience.

- WSG reference: Remove unnecessary code (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-code)

## WSG Defer Non-Critical Resources Analysis

- WSG reference: Defer the loading of non-critical resources (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#defer-the-loading-of-non-critical-resources)
- Non-critical score: 0 (few defer opportunities) to 100 (many deferrable resources)
- Uses offscreen image savings, unused CSS/JS, render-blocking delay, and heavy likely-non-critical assets

## WSG Modularization Analysis

- WSG reference: Modularize bandwidth-heavy components (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#modularize-bandwidth-heavy-components)
- Modularization score: 0 (few obvious on-demand opportunities) to 100 (many heavy initial-load components)
- This analysis looks for heavy scripts, images, styles, fonts, and media that should be split or loaded on demand.

## Cross-Page Patterns

- This same heavy image pattern appears on 99 pages. Fix it once in the shared template, CMS block, or design system component to reduce load across the whole site.
- These 1 script bundles are recurring modularization targets. Move the fix to the shared bundle or component boundary so every affected page gets lighter at once.
- These 3 modularization targets recur across multiple pages. Centralize the fix at the component or bundle level instead of treating each page separately.
- Heavy assets from administracion.gob.es recur across 100 pages. Review whether that dependency should be deferred, optimized, or loaded conditionally.

### Recurring Heavy Assets

- script assets from www.googletagmanager.com/gtag appears on 100 pages (25.70 MB total)
- stylesheet assets from administracion.gob.es/resources appears on 99 pages (16.63 MB total)
- image assets from administracion.gob.es/resources appears on 99 pages (15.10 MB total)
- script assets from administracion.gob.es/ruxitagentjs_ICA7NVfgqrux_10331260218130851.js appears on 99 pages (7.95 MB total)
- script assets from administracion.gob.es/resources appears on 98 pages (25.87 MB total)

### Recurring Modularization Targets

- Split JavaScript by route or interaction affects 100 pages (est. 54.15 MB aggregate savings)
- Modularize heavy stylesheet asset from administracion.gob.es affects 100 pages (est. 17.75 MB aggregate savings)
- Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries affects 100 pages (est. 10.35 MB aggregate savings)
- Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render affects 100 pages
- Modularize heavy image asset from administracion.gob.es affects 99 pages (est. 16.39 MB aggregate savings)

### Recurring Heavy Origins

- administracion.gob.es is a recurring heavy source on 100 pages (69.86 MB total)
- www.googletagmanager.com is a recurring heavy source on 100 pages (25.70 MB total)

## Site-Wide Guidance

- This scan appears to target a single site. Shared recommendations below should typically be fixed once at platform/template level.

### https://administracion.gob.es
- Sampled pages: 100
- Shared recommendations:
  - [LOW] Publish .well-known/security.txt
    - .well-known/security.txt is missing for all sampled pages on https://administracion.gob.es.
  - [HIGH] Apply Content-Security-Policy site-wide
    - CSP is absent across all sampled pages on https://administracion.gob.es.

    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [HIGH] Modularize heavy image asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 487.14 KB)
    - Serve a smaller placeholder first and load this asset only when the user scrolls to or requests the component.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 1 request(s), 139.71 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [LOW] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.00 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 442.32 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [HIGH] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 258.29 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 442.32 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.00 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5501 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy image from administracion.gob.es (est. 487.14 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 70 small targets on mobile and 119 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 4 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 683.28 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 412.36 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 29.76 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 683.28 KB.
  - [HIGH] Right-size images for viewport
    - Avoid sending larger images than the rendered size needs. Estimated byte savings: 475.19 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 442.32 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 412.36 KB.
  - [HIGH] Lazy-load offscreen images
    - Defer below-the-fold imagery until it is likely to be viewed. Estimated byte savings: 258.29 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.00 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 2.15 MB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 15.0 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index

  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)

- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 100.47 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 564.38 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.29 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.86 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 564.38 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 100.47 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 564.38 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.86 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5369 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 38 small targets on mobile and 69 on tablet.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 564.38 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5369 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 999.71 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.9 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)

    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.

    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.

    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.

    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.

    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.

    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.

    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.

    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.

    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.50 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5395 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 999.71 KB).

- Heavy initial-load requests: 8 (1.09 MB total)
- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 100.47 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 563.61 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.86 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 563.61 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 100.47 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 563.61 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.86 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5364 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 40 small targets on mobile and 66 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 1 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.61 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5364 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 999.74 KB).

    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 562.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.88 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5144 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 872.07 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.6 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index
  - Current value: 6.7 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused JavaScript
  - Current value: Est savings of 563 KiB
  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused CSS
  - Current value: Est savings of 168 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources

- Heavy initial-load requests: 6 (936.96 KB total)
- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 88.98 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 548.04 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.09 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 168.14 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 548.04 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 88.98 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 548.04 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.14 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 4834 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.

  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 28 small targets on mobile and 61 on tablet.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 562.93 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.18 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4579 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 851.65 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.3 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index
  - Current value: 6.8 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused JavaScript
  - Current value: Est savings of 563 KiB
  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused CSS
  - Current value: Est savings of 168 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
  - Current value: Est savings of 4,580 ms
  - WSG: 18 Use the most efficient solution for your service (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-the-most-efficient-solution-for-your-service)
- [HIGH] Enable text compression
  - Current value: Est savings of 285 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)

- Heavy initial-load requests: 6 (936.99 KB total)
- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 102.47 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 563.42 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.88 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 563.42 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 102.47 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 563.42 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.88 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5065 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.

    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 88.98 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 548.16 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.06 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 4822 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 30 small targets on mobile and 60 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 2 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 548.16 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.06 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4822 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 853.32 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.3 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index
  - Current value: 7.5 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused JavaScript
  - Current value: Est savings of 548 KiB
  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused CSS
  - Current value: Est savings of 168 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
  - Current value: Est savings of 4,820 ms
  - WSG: 18 Use the most efficient solution for your service (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-the-most-efficient-solution-for-your-service)
- [HIGH] Enable text compression

  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 73.65 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 562.92 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 168.28 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 562.92 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 73.65 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 562.92 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.28 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 4599 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 25 small targets on mobile and 52 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 2 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 562.92 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.28 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4599 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 851.73 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.2 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index
  - Current value: 6.6 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused JavaScript
  - Current value: Est savings of 563 KiB
  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused CSS
  - Current value: Est savings of 168 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
  - Current value: Est savings of 4,600 ms
  - WSG: 18 Use the most efficient solution for your service (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-the-most-efficient-solution-for-your-service)
- [HIGH] Enable text compression
  - Current value: Est savings of 285 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)

- Estimated redundant transfer: 735.35 KB (58.6% of transfer)
- Redundancy recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Reduce unused javascript (est. 567.50 KB savings)
  - [HIGH] Reduce unused css (est. 167.84 KB savings)
  - [MEDIUM] Enable and verify text compression (est. 285.11 KB savings)
- Heavy initial-load requests: 6 (937.00 KB total)
- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 102.52 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 567.50 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.84 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 567.50 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 102.52 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 567.50 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.84 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5057 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 51 small targets on mobile and 82 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 1 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 567.50 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.84 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5057 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 872.11 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 8.0 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index
  - Current value: 8.7 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused JavaScript
  - Current value: Est savings of 568 KiB
  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)
- [HIGH] Reduce unused CSS
  - Current value: Est savings of 168 KiB
  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
  - Current value: Est savings of 5,060 ms
  - WSG: 18 Use the most efficient solution for your service (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-the-most-efficient-solution-for-your-service)
- [HIGH] Enable text compression

- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 88.98 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 548.89 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 168.17 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 548.89 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 88.98 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 548.89 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.17 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 4575 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 31 small targets on mobile and 60 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 6 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 548.89 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.17 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4575 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 853.32 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.5 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)

  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 837.57 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.2 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)

- Heavy initial-load requests: 6 (937.00 KB total)
- On-demand modularization candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 88.98 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 563.77 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.12 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 168.19 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 563.77 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 88.98 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 563.77 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.19 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 4797 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.

    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.

  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.

    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 37 small targets on mobile and 75 on tablet.

    - 5 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.

    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.

    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.

  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
